data.title: Truckmaker Volvo Group's Q1 operating profit lags forecast
data.description:
Truckmaker Volvo Group reported a bigger drop than expected in first-quarter operating profit on Thursday but increased the market outlook forecast for Europe truck registrations.

Operating profit was 10.68 billion Swedish crowns ($1.15 billion) against a year-earlier 13.26 billion and an average expectation among analysts of 11.39 billion in an LSEG poll.

Volvo raised its forecast for this year's overall European heavy truck market to 310,000 units from 305,000 seen in January, while maintaining the outlook for North American heavy truck sales at 265,000 vehicles.

($1 = 9.2673 Swedish crowns)

data.title: Renesas Electronics Corp Bottom Line Rises In Q1
data.description:
TOKYO (dpa-AFX) - Renesas Electronics Corp (RNECF) announced a profit for its first quarter that Increased, from the same period last year

The company's earnings totaled JPY68.149 billion, or JPY36.96 per share. This compares with JPY26.006 billion, or JPY14.30 per share, last year.

The company's revenue for the period rose 23.2% to JPY380.293 billion from JPY308.777 billion last year.

-Earnings: JPY68.149 Bln. vs. JPY26.006 Bln. last year.

-EPS: JPY36.96 vs. JPY14.30 last year.

-Revenue: JPY380.293 Bln vs. JPY308.777 Bln last year.

data.title: Appliances maker Electrolux swings to loss in Q1 as demand slumps in North America
data.description:
Swedish home appliances maker Electrolux reported on Friday a surprise operating loss for the first quarter on slowing sales as demand in North America slumped.

Electrolux said s ales in North America shrank organically by 11.6%, mainly reflecting weaker market conditions, leading to a group organic sales decline of 0.5%.

The group late on Thursday announced it would partner with Chinese rival Midea in North America, where Electrolux has been struggling for years. It also announced a 9 billion crown ($971 million) rights issue to fund the partnership, as well as job cuts and other cost cutting measures.

Electrolux, whose brands also include Frigidaire and AEG, also said late on Thursday that operating profit before non-recurring items in the first quarter landed at around 200 million crowns, missing analysts' expectations of 781 million, on sales of 30 billion.

Including one-off items, it swung to an operating loss of 266 million crowns from a year-earlier profit of 452 million, the full earnings report showed on Friday. Analysts had before Thursday's news on average expected a profit of 280 million crowns, according to a poll provided by Electrolux.

($1 = 9.2663 Swedish crowns)

data.title: Signify's first-quarter sales fall due to weak US dollar
data.description:
The world's biggest lighting maker Signify reported a 12% drop in its quarterly nominal sales to 1.27 billion euros ($1.48 billion) on Friday, dragged by currency effects related to the depreciation of the U.S. dollar.

The Dutch company confirmed its full-year guidance for a core profit margin of between 7.5% and 8%, but declined to provide a sales forecast.

Operating in more than 70 markets with brands such as Philips, Philips Hue, and Color Kinetics, the company faces sluggish global lighting demand while navigating a restructuring programme launched earlier this year. A strategic portfolio review is also underway to reposition Signify for growth.

Its net income plunged to 8 million euros from 67 million euros a year ago, mainly due to restructuring charges of 63 million euros linked to the 180-million-euro cost reduction plan announced in January.

Signify reduced its workforce to 26,008 employees by the end of the quarter from 29,697 a year earlier, with most of the cuts tied to factory roles as production volumes dipped.

($1 = 0.8563 euros)

data.title: Amazon-Backed Nuclear Tech Firm X-Energy Raises $1 Billion in U.S. IPO
data.description:
By Kimberley Kao

Nuclear reactor developer X-Energy raised $1.02 billion in its U.S. initial public offering, coming as the artificial-intelligence boom drives demand for more power sources.

The Amazon-backed company said Thursday that it sold 44.3 million shares in an upsized offering at $23 each, above the guidance range of $16 to $19.

That implies a valuation of more than $9 billion, according to calculations based on prior exchange filings. Shares are expected to start trading on the Nasdaq on Friday.

The listing comes as the frenzied buildout of AI infrastructure fuels a massive need for more electricity to run energy-hungry data centers. As the technology advances, more and more companies are considering nuclear as a power source.

The first commercial nuclear-power projects in a decade are currently under construction in the U.S., including one by Bill Gates-backed TerraPower.

Facebook parent Meta recently said it plans to back nuclear projects with Oklo, TerraPower and Vistra, with other deals including Microsoft's power-purchase agreement with Constellation Energy to spur the restart of a nuclear plant in Pennsylvania, and a Google-NextEra Energy partnership to reopen a reactor in Iowa.

X-Energy said it is well-placed to capitalize on the rising demand from data centers, seeing scope for a big market for small modular reactors--compact "plug and play" versions of nuclear reactors that use fission to create electricity.

The company plans to use the proceeds from the IPO for working capital, future projects and general purposes like research and development.

In November last year, X-Energy closed a Series D financing round of about $700 million led by Jane Street, and including investors like Cathie Wood's ARK Invest.

In 2024, Amazon anchored a financing round of about $500 million for X-Energy, which said at the time that it was working with the tech giant to bring more than 5 gigawatts of new power projects online across the U.S. by 2039.

Founded in 2009, X-Energy develops advanced small modular nuclear reactors and fuel technology for clean energy generation.

Write to Kimberley Kao at kimberley.kao@wsj.com

data.title: Holcim Q1 Recurring EBIT Up 8.3% Organically; Confirms FY26 Guidance
data.description:
JONA (dpa-AFX) - Holcim (HLB.F, HOLN.SW) reported net sales of 3.52 billion Swiss francs in the first quarter, up 3.9% organically compared to the prior-year period, with momentum accelerating in March. Recurring EBIT grew 8.3% organically to 431 million francs, from the prior-year period.

Holcim said its NextGen Growth 2030 strategy is expected to continue to drive superior performance and value creation. Building on its strong results to start the year, Holcim confirmed its fiscal 2026 guidance.

At last close, Holcim shares were trading at 71.88 Swiss francs, up 0.62%.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: U.S. Treasury Yields Edge Up as Oil Price Remain High — Market Talk
data.description: U.S. Treasury yields and the oil price edge higher in Asian trade with no meaningful progress between the U.S. and Iran toward a peace deal, even as neither seeks a re-escalation of the tensions. "U.S. Treasurys sold off a touch as oil drifted higher despite the ongoing cease-fire," Barclays Anshut Pradhan and Demi Hu say in a note. The two-year Treasury yield rises 1.7 basis points to 3.841%, while the 10-year yield is up 0.7 basis points to 4.329%, according to Tradeweb. (emese.bartha@wsj.com)
